content.problemStatement:
Kattapa, as you all know was one of the greatest warriors of his time. The kingdom of Maahishmati had never lost a battle under him (as army-chief), and the reason for that was their really powerful army, also called as **Mahasena**.

Kattapa was known to be a very superstitious person. He believed that a soldier is "lucky" if the soldier is holding an **even number** of weapons, and "unlucky" otherwise. He considered the army as "READY FOR BATTLE" if the count of "lucky" soldiers is strictly greater than the count of "unlucky" soldiers, and "NOT READY" otherwise.

Given the number of weapons each soldier is holding, your task is to determine whether the army formed by all these soldiers is "READY FOR BATTLE" or "NOT READY".

**Note**: You can find the definition of an even number here.


Input Format

The first line of input consists of a single integer **N** denoting the number of soldiers. The second line of input consists of **N** space separated integers **A1**, **A2**, ..., **AN**, where **Ai** denotes the number of weapons that the **i**th soldier is holding.



Output Format
Generate one line output saying "READY FOR BATTLE", if the army satisfies the conditions that Kattapa requires or "NOT READY" otherwise (quotes for clarity).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (the number of soldiers). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, where Ai is the number of weapons held by the i‑th soldier.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing either "READY FOR BATTLE" if the number of soldiers holding an even number of weapons is strictly greater than those holding an odd number, or "NOT READY" otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case, N=1, the only soldier holds 1 weapon (odd). Even count = 0, odd count = 1 → NOT READY.
One test case, N=1, the only soldier holds 2 weapons (even). Even count = 1, odd count = 0 → READY FOR BATTLE.
First test case: even soldiers = 2 (12,14), odd = 2 (11,13) → NOT READY. Second test case: even = 2 (2,4), odd = 1 (3) → READY FOR BATTLE.

hidden.label: multiple test cases
hidden.expectedOutput: READY FOR BATTLE\nNOT READY\nREADY FOR BATTLE

solution.approach: For each test case we simply count how many Ai are even and how many are odd. If evenCount > oddCount we print "READY FOR BATTLE", otherwise we print "NOT READY". The algorithm is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
